hypothesis: A direct index is proven only after the exact len-derived exit guard and only until a relevant mutation or nested-control frontier.

measurement.note: Structured per-site compiler accounting is the proof oracle; LLVM may independently remove obvious checks in optimized IR.

cases.hypothesis: A len-derived i >= m exit guard proves the following direct b[i] read in bounds.

expected.relative: facts removes the raw bounds edge; LLVM may independently remove the control edge

cases.hypothesis: The same dominating guard proves a direct b[i] write in bounds.

expected.relative: facts removes the raw bounds edge

cases.hypothesis: A non-mutating nested match preserves the outer guard's dominance for b[i].

expected.relative: facts removes the raw bounds edge inside the match arm

cases.hypothesis: Mutation after the indexed access does not invalidate that access's dominating fact.

expected.relative: facts removes the raw check before the mutation frontier

cases.hypothesis: A length from a cannot prove an access into b.

expected.relative: facts must not remove the bounds edge

cases.hypothesis: A copied-but-equivalent bound is not accepted by the deliberately structural v1 prover.

expected.relative: raw check remains; backend may prove it independently

cases.hypothesis: An i > m exit condition leaves i == m reachable and cannot prove safety.

expected.relative: facts must not remove the reachable trap

cases.hypothesis: A guard proving i < m does not prove i + 1 < m.

expected.relative: facts must retain the derived-index trap

cases.hypothesis: Changing i after the guard invalidates the fact before b[i].

expected.relative: facts must stop at the mutation frontier

cases.hypothesis: Changing the len-derived bound invalidates its provenance before b[i].

expected.relative: facts must retain the check when the bound is mutable

cases.hypothesis: The v1 structural prover conservatively stops at nested loop control.

expected.relative: raw check remains; backend may preserve dominance through the nested loop

cases.hypothesis: A guard appearing after b[i] does not dominate the access.

expected.relative: facts must retain the reachable pre-guard trap
